mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate negative equity repricing over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of triggered months, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE changes are below the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in over 60% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of low inflation reflects healthy capacity utilization without price pressures, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3% annualized, or the signal fails to predict direction in more than 50% of low-inflation months.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, high borrowing costs suppress corporate earnings growth expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns dominate in more than 65% of high-rate months, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls alongside a declining unemployment rate confirms robust job creation that absorbs slack, producing positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of observations, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive value over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate revenue expectations, creating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 prices over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 2-year windows, or forward returns show zero correlation with quarterly GDP revisions.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production during a monetary tightening phase signals an impending recession, triggering immediate negative equity repricing over one week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in more than 60% of contraction months during tightening, or the signal fails to separate from market noise in walk-forward tests.
